issues.description: Path resolution injects a spurious 'imagens/' segment before arquivo_path. The actual relative path stored in DB by adicionarImagem() is 'images/products/{codigo}/{filename}' (see imagens.ts line 35). So path.join(getDataDir(), 'imagens', item.arquivo_path) produces {dataDir}/imagens/images/products/{codigo}/{filename} which does not exist — every readFile call in the batch will throw ENOENT and every item will be counted as a failure.
issues.fix_required: Change line 79 to: const arquivoPath = path.isAbsolute(item.arquivo_path) ? item.arquivo_path : path.join(getDataDir(), item.arquivo_path). This matches the correct pattern used in imagens.ts (removerImagem line 138, atribuirAProduto line 93) and in the vision.analisar IPC handler (tipc.ts line 676-677).

suggestions.description: totalAcima80 counts results without excluding rejected items, so the 'Aceitar todos > 80% (N)' button count can be misleading — e.g. showing '5' but only 3 will actually be accepted because 2 were rejected. Consider filtering rejeitados out of the count: Array.from(resultados.entries()).filter(([produto_id, r]) => r.confianca > 80 && !rejeitados.has(produto_id)).length

Discovery risk note: batch handler does not guard against OpenRouter models that may not support vision. If the configured model is an OpenRouter non-vision model, the Gemini vision call will fail per-item (caught gracefully). Consider logging a warning when provider is 'openrouter' and model does not start with 'google/' to aid debugging.
